content:
type: SURVEY
files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Adding register_filter(name, func)/unregister_filter(name) global filter registry; modifying Prompt._template_from_str and _template_from_file to merge global registry filters into env.filters (local/built-in filters take precedence on name collision).

content:
type: DEFER
files: outlines/prompts.py
spec: Please own outlines/prompts.py and implement my feature too: add a module-level global filter registry, e.g. `_FILTERS: Dict[str, Callable] = {}`, plus two public functions: `register_filter(name: str, func: Callable) -> None` which stores `_FILTERS[name] = func`, and `unregister_filter(name: str) -> None` which does `_FILTERS.pop(name, None)` (no error if missing). Wherever you build/populate the Jinja2 Environment's filters (your create_jinja_env() helper, or _template_from_str/_template_from_file), first apply the registered global filters (env.filters.update(_FILTERS)), THEN apply the built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args) and any locally-passed filters (your  param) so local/built-in filters override global ones on name collision. Order matters: global filters applied first, local/built-in filters applied after/override. Since the registry is mutable at runtime (register_filter/unregister_filter can be called after templates were created), the environment's filters dict should be refreshed from _FILTERS each time a template is rendered/created rather than cached statically once at import time -- e.g. re-apply env.filters.update(_FILTERS) each time create_jinja_env() (or equivalent) runs, so newly registered/unregistered filters are picked up. No changes needed to the Prompt class's public call signature for this feature. I will not edit outlines/prompts.py myself.
note: register_filter/unregister_filter global registry feature

content:
type: DEFER
files: outlines/prompts.py
spec: Global filter registry feature spec for outlines/prompts.py:

1. Module-level global registry: _FILTERS: Dict[str, Callable] = {} (add Dict import if not already present; Callable already imported).

2. Public function register_filter(name: str, func: Callable) -> None:
   - Stores func in the registry under name: _FILTERS[name] = func.
   - No validation needed beyond that.

3. Public function unregister_filter(name: str) -> None:
   - Removes name from the registry if present: _FILTERS.pop(name, None). Must not raise if name is not registered.

4. Merge into Jinja2 Environment filters, in both _template_from_str and _template_from_file (or your shared create_jinja_env() helper):
   - Apply registered global filters first: env.filters.update(_FILTERS)
   - Then apply the built-in filters (name, description, source, signature, schema, args) and any locally-passed filters (your filters param) AFTER, so they override on name collision. i.e. built-in/local filters take precedence over global registry filters with the same name.
   - Because register_filter/unregister_filter can be called at any time (including after a Prompt object already exists), env.filters.update(_FILTERS) should be re-applied every time a template's Jinja2 Environment is constructed (i.e. each call to _template_from_str/_template_from_file or create_jinja_env), not cached once at import time. Since these methods already construct a fresh jinja2.Environment per call, this happens naturally as long as you pull from the live _FILTERS dict at that point rather than a snapshot copy.

5. No changes needed to Prompt's public call signature for this feature specifically.

I will not edit outlines/prompts.py myself -- this is fully deferred to you.
note: register_filter/unregister_filter global registry feature
